scope: Sibling mesh runtime sessions (Drive-G-1, SovereignX, ops-console, and related transports).
purpose: Define the constitutional conditions under which live sibling federation is permitted, distinguishing checkout authority from live mesh authority.

invariants.name: No live authority without receipt
invariants.rule: A sibling remote MUST NOT be treated as federated unless a valid handshake receipt exists and is attached to the session lineage.

invariants.name: Adapters are necessary but not sufficient
invariants.rule: Verified adapter contracts prove checkout authority only. They do not grant live mesh authority without a handshake.

invariants.name: Fail-closed by default
invariants.rule: Absent a verified adapter, declared transport descriptor, or valid handshake receipt, the federation decision MUST resolve to deny.

invariants.name: Lineage-backed sessions
invariants.rule: Every live session must have a sessionId, a lineage record (adapter, transport, receipt), and a revocation path.

constitutionalGuarantee: Live mesh authority is granted only when a verified adapter, declared transport, and valid handshake receipt are all present. Checkout adapter pass alone never grants FederationGranted.
promotionConditions: At least one transport is Tested or Certified (tested=true)
Indexed valid handshake receipts with receiptId, sessionId, remoteId, adapterId, transportId, issuedAt, expiresAt, receiptHash
Active session lineage entries with sessionId, remoteId, adapterId, transportId, receiptId and linked non-empty revocationPath
Grant events recorded as Active (not Pending-only); revocation events recordable via revokeSession
truthBoundary: This CCR publishes federation decision machinery and deny-by-default evaluation. It does not claim any live FederationGranted session until a valid handshake receipt is present and indexed.
